meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement user onboarding to overcome the 'can't finish projects' hurdle. A beginner tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: You struggle to finish projects because the feedback loop is too long. By implementing a high-impact user onboarding flow, you force yourself to define the core value proposition immediately. This narrows your scope, prevents feature creep, and provides a tangible milestone that keeps you motivated to cross the finish line.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished projects is littered with grand visions that lacked a clear point of entry. You start with high energy, only to get lost in the weeds of complex backend logic, eventually abandoning the codebase when the sheer weight of 'everything' becomes overwhelming. It is not a lack of talent; it is a lack of focus.

True mastery comes from shipping. By designing a frictionless user onboarding experience, you shift your focus from 'building a system' to 'delivering a moment.' This constraint acts as a lighthouse, guiding your development process and ensuring you build only what matters to get the user from 'hello' to 'value' in record time.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the single task that provides immediate value to your user. This prevents you from over-engineering the dashboard before the user has even started.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my app's core value proposition and suggest the shortest possible path for a user to experience their first success. Keep it to 3 steps or less.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project README and identify the single most important user action.'

steps.title: Build a Linear Onboarding Flow
steps.description: Create a simple, non-branching sequence that guides the user through the 'Aha!' moment. Avoid complex conditional logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the React component structure for a 3-step linear onboarding wizard that collects user preferences and leads to the main dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new onboarding component set with simple state management for step progression.'

steps.title: Implement 'Empty State' Guidance
steps.description: Don't leave the screen blank. Use onboarding to prompt the user to take the first action, which makes the app feel 'alive' instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft copy and UI placeholders for an empty state dashboard that encourages the user to complete their first project task.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Update the empty dashboard state to include a clear call-to-action button and an instructional illustration.'

steps.title: Automate Data Seeding
steps.description: Let users see what the app looks like with data immediately. It drastically increases perceived value and keeps them engaged.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to seed the database with 3 dummy items upon user registration so the UI doesn't look empty.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a seed script that populates the database with sample user data for new accounts.'

steps.title: Instrument Success Tracking
steps.description: Add simple logging to see if users actually complete the onboarding. This data will tell you if your project is actually usable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a simple event listener to the onboarding completion button to log the success to the console.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add console.log analytics tracking to the onboarding completion handler.'

pro_tips: Never build an onboarding flow that takes more than 60 seconds to complete.
Use 'progressive disclosure'—only ask for data when you absolutely need it to provide value.
If you can't describe your onboarding in one sentence, your project scope is too big.
Always include a 'Skip' button. Forcing users to learn your UI is a fast way to lose them.
Treat your onboarding as the most important feature; if users don't pass this, the rest of your app doesn't exist.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding is live, deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify and share it with three friends to observe where they get stuck. Use that feedback to iterate, not to add new features.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building aimlessly and start shipping products that users love. Focus on the first 60 seconds, and the rest will follow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
